You are an adversarial production-readiness assessor for the hc-assay engine at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (a dataset-agnostic, data-sovereign empirical-ML/methodology engine; optional backends are import-guarded; transcripts are committed as provenance and scrubbed of operator PII intentionally). This codebase has already survived SEVEN hardening passes — so easy findings are gone. Report ONLY genuine, concrete, FALSIFIABLE production-readiness defects with a real failure mode; no style nits, no speculative "could be nicer". Each finding needs a file:line/symbol location and a claim someone could disprove by reading the code. Prefer depth over breadth. If a dimension is genuinely clean, return few or zero findings — do NOT invent. Read the actual code (use Read/Grep/Bash; run tests if useful — note: use "python -m pytest", and "source .venv/bin/activate").

YOUR DIMENSION: observability/provenance.
Tracing/metrics/tracking correctness, MLflow lifecycle (orphaned runs, status), structured logging (does it leak secrets/PII into logs?), env-var parsing, provenance artifact completeness and integrity.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
69	        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
70	        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
71	        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
72	        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
73	        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
74	        try:
75	            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
76	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
77	            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
78	            if exp is not None:
79	                return str(exp.experiment_id)
80	            raise
81	
82	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
83	        client = self._client()
84	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
85	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
86	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
87	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
88	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
89	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
90	        try:
91	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
92	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
93	        except Exception:
94	            try:
95	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
96	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
97	                pass
98	            raise
99	        return run_id
100	
101	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
102	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
103	
104	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
105	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
106	
107	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
108	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
109	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
110	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
111

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	
31	
32	def _int_env(name: str, default: str) -> int:
33	    """Parse an int env var with a clear error naming the var (#CV-S-1, same class as #H-022).
34	
35	    A bare ``int(os.environ[...])`` at import time raises an opaque "invalid literal for int()"
36	    that names neither the var nor fires inside any handler.
37	    """
38	    raw = os.environ.get(name, default)
39	    try:
40	        return int(raw)
41	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
42	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an integer; got {raw!r}") from exc
43	
44	
45	TRACING_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006")
46	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
47	
48	_provider: Any = None
49	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
50	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
51	
52	
53	def _disabled() -> bool:
54	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
55	
56	
57	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
58	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
59	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
60	
61	
62	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
63	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
64	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
65	
66	
67	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
68	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
69	
70	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
71	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
72	    """
73	    global _provider
74	    if _disabled():
75	        return None
76	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
77	    with _provider_lock:
78	        if _provider is not None:
79	            return _provider
80	        try:
81	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
82	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
83	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
84	            from phoenix.otel import register
85	
86	            provider = register(
87	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
88	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
89	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
90	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
91	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
92	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
93	            )
94	            _provider = provider
95	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
96	            _instrument_langchain()
97	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
98	            return _provider
99	        except Exception as exc:
100	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
101	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
102	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
103	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
104	            _log.warning(
105	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
106	                type(exc).__name__,
107	                exc,
108	            )
109	            return None
110	
111	
112	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
113	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
114	    try:
115	        from opentelemetry import trace
116	
117	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
118	            _log.warning(
119	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
120	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
121	            )
122	    except Exception:
123	        pass
124	
125	
126	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
127	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
128	    try:
129	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
130	
131	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
132	    except Exception:
133	        pass
134	
135	
136	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
137	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
138	
139	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
140	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
141	    """
142	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
143	    if not callable(flush):
144	        return
145	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
146	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
147	        try:
148	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
149	
150	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
151	                _safe(flush)
152	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
153	                if callable(prior):
154	                    prior(signum, frame)
155	
156	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
157	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
158	            pass
159	
160	
161	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
162	    try:
163	        fn()
164	    except Exception:
165	        pass
166	
167	
168	@contextmanager
169	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
170	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
171	
172	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
173	    """
174	    if run_id is None:
175	        yield
176	        return
177	    try:
178	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
179	    except ImportError:
180	        yield
181	        return
182	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
183	    try:
184	        yield
185	    finally:
186	        context.detach(token)
187	
188	
189	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
190	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
191	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
192	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
193	
194	
195	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
196	class Tracer(Protocol):
197	    @contextmanager
198	    def span(
199	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
200	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
201	
202	
203	class OtelTracer:
204	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
205	
206	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
207	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
208	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
209	    """
210	
211	    @contextmanager
212	    def span(
213	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
214	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
215	        try:
216	            from opentelemetry import trace
217	        except ImportError:
218	            yield
219	            return
220	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
221	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
222	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
223	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
224	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
225	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
226	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
227	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
228	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
229	            yield
230	
231	
232	class UnconfiguredTracer:
233	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
234	
235	    @contextmanager
236	    def span(
237	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
238	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
239	        raise NotImplementedError(
240	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
241	            "test double"
242	        )
243	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
244

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266	
267	def from_records(
268	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
269	    *,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
272	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
273	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
274	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
275	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
276	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
277	        if missing:
278	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
279	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
280	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
281	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
282	        rebuilt.append(
283	            ProvenanceEntry(
284	                seq=r["seq"],
285	                kind=r["kind"],
286	                summary=r["summary"],
287	                payload=r["payload"],
288	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
289	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
290	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
291	            )
292	        )
293	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
294	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
295	    return entries
296

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
53	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
54	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
55	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
56	]
57	
58	
59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
77	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
78	
79	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
80	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
81	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
82	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
83	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
84	    over-redacting common words.
85	    """
86	    names: list[str] = []
87	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
88	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
89	    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
90	    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
91	    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
92	    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
93	    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
94	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
95	        if v:
96	            names.append(v)
97	    try:
98	        import subprocess
99	
100	        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
101	            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
102	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
103	                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
104	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
105	        pass
106	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
107	    seen: set[str] = set()
108	    for n in names:
109	        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
110	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
111	        seen.add(n)
112	        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
113	        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
114	        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
115	        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
116	        # CASE-INSENSITIVE (#J-002 confirm-concern round 2): the operator's name also appears
117	        # lowercased as actor/handle tokens — "[REDACTED]@hcgrc" (an initiated_by field), "[REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
118	        # (human)" in actor tables — which a case-sensitive pattern misses. re.IGNORECASE covers all
119	        # casings; "Joneses"/"joneses" stays protected by the trailing \b either way.
120	        toks = n.split()
121	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in toks) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
122	        # Redact each NAME TOKEN standalone too (#J-002 confirm-concern): the full-name pattern
123	        # leaves the operator's bare first/last name in cleartext — e.g. "[REDACTED] approves ..." and
124	        # "[REDACTED] builds and governs ..." appear in governance prose hundreds of times, uniquely
125	        # re-identifying the operator. Per-token redaction (len>=4, trailing \b, no leading \b for
126	        # the JSON-\n-glued case) closes it. Over-redaction is bounded: the tokens come only from
127	        # the verified operator name and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of these tokens
128	        # (all 256 "[REDACTED]" contexts were sampled — every one is an operator governance reference).
129	        for t in toks:
130	            if len(t) >= 4 and t.lower() not in seen:
131	                seen.add(t.lower())
132	                pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(t) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
133	    return pats
134	
135	
136	def _repo_handle_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
137	    """Redactions for the operator's repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008).
138	
139	    The handle (the ``<owner>`` in ``github.com/<owner>/<repo>``, e.g. in PR/issue URLs and
140	    ``gh --repo <owner>/...`` commands) is operator-identifying PII that NO existing rule covers:
141	    it is not an email (the email rule needs an ``@``), and it differs from the home-dir username
142	    the bare-username rule derives. Left uncovered it leaked across hundreds of transcript files
143	    (#J-008). Derive candidate handles from the git remotes and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES`` env;
144	    redact each as a bare token (length-guarded). A handle is a single unambiguous string, so no
145	    word-boundary is needed — redact it inside URLs, ``gh`` args, and the ``<handle>@`` email stem.
146	    Matched case-insensitively, and the distinctive STEM (handle minus a generic ``the`` prefix) is
147	    also redacted, because the handle leaks via derived names — e.g. ``[REDACTED]-audience-profiles``
148	    (a skill name) embeds the handle minus ``the`` (#J-008 confirm-concern).
149	    """
150	    handles: list[str] = []
151	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES", "")
152	    handles.extend(h.strip() for h in extra.split(",") if h.strip())
153	    try:
154	        import subprocess
155	
156	        out = subprocess.run(["git", "remote", "-v"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
157	        if out.returncode == 0:
158	            for m in re.finditer(r"github\.com[:/]([^/\s]+)/", out.stdout):
159	                handles.append(m.group(1))
160	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
161	        pass
162	    # Expand each handle with its distinctive stem (drop a leading generic "the") so handle-derived
163	    # names/slugs are covered too (#J-008 confirm-concern).
164	    expanded: list[str] = []
165	    for h in handles:
166	        expanded.append(h)
167	        if h.lower().startswith("the") and len(h) - 3 >= 4:
168	            expanded.append(h[3:])
169	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
170	    seen: set[str] = set()
171	    for h in expanded:
172	        key = h.lower()
173	        if len(h) < 4 or key in seen:
174	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
175	        seen.add(key)
176	        if len(h) >= 8:
177	            # SEPARATOR-FLEXIBLE (#J-008 confirm-concern round 3): a GitHub handle is usually a
178	            # de-spaced brand ("[REDACTED]" = "[REDACTED]"); the SPACED source brand
179	            # appears in prose/links and lets a reader reconstruct the redacted handle/email by
180	            # concatenation. Allow punctuation/whitespace between each character so the spaced,
181	            # hyphenated, and dotted forms all redact — while the de-spaced form (empty separators)
182	            # still matches. The separator class is space/._- ONLY, so unrelated prose cannot
183	            # accidentally spell out the handle, and common single tokens (e.g. "CISO") are NOT
184	            # redacted because the WHOLE letter sequence (…[REDACTED]) must be present in order.
185	            pats.append(re.compile(r"[\s._-]*".join(re.escape(c) for c in h), re.IGNORECASE))
186	        else:
187	            pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(h), re.IGNORECASE))
188	    return pats

196	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
197	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
198	        if pat.groups == 2:
199	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
200	        elif pat.groups == 1:
201	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
202	        else:
203	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
204	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
205	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
206	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
207	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
208	    # Redact the operator's full display name + bare name tokens too (#J-002) — distinct PII the
209	    # username/email rules leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
210	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
211	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
212	    # Redact the repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008) — operator-identifying PII in PR/issue URLs
213	    # and `gh --repo <handle>/...` commands that no email/username rule covers.
214	    for pat in _REPO_HANDLE_RES:
215	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
216	    return text

219	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
220	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
221	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
222	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
223	
224	
225	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
226	    try:
227	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
228	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
229	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
230	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
231	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
232	        #
233	        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
234	        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
235	        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
236	        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
237	        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
238	        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
239	        # present) is copied verbatim.
240	        if b"\x00" in raw:
241	            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
242	        else:
243	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
244	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
245	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
246	        else:
247	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
248	        return {
249	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
250	            "bytes": len(data),
251	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
252	        }
253	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
254	        return None
255	
256	
257	def main() -> int:
258	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
259	    if not src.is_dir():

261	    session = _active_session(src)
262	    if session is None:
263	        return 0
264	    sid = session.stem
265	    captured: list[dict] = []
266	
267	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
268	    if rec:
269	        captured.append(rec)
270	
271	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
272	    subtree = src / sid
273	    if subtree.is_dir():
274	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
275	            if f.is_file():
276	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
277	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
278	                if rec:
279	                    captured.append(rec)
280	
281	    try:
282	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
283	        MANIFEST.write_text(
284	            json.dumps(
285	                {
286	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
287	                    "active_session": sid,
288	                    "n_files": len(captured),
289	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
290	                    "files": captured,
291	                },
292	                indent=2,
293	            )
294	            + "\n",
295	            encoding="utf-8",
296	        )
297	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
298	        return 0
299	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
300	    return 0
301	
302	
303	if __name__ == "__main__":
304	    sys.exit(main())

Confirmed second defect: if `json.dump(entries_to_records(...))` raises (line 743) — which can happen because `entries_to_records` deep-thaws payloads to plain dicts but payloads could contain non-JSON-serializable values that survived freezing (e.g. a custom object recorded in a gate evidence dict) — then `path` was assigned but the `with` block exits via exception. `delete=False` means the temp file persists, and the cleanup `try/finally` at 748 is never reached because the exception propagates first. The file leaks on disk with provenance contents. The `track()` wrapper catches the exception (so the run continues) but the temp file is orphaned.

Wait — `path = fh.name` is assigned AFTER `json.dump` on the same line-sequence; if `json.dump` raises, `path` is never assigned. So the leak is the NamedTemporaryFile itself (created on `__enter__`, `delete=False` so not auto-removed on exception exit). The cleanup code references `path` which was never assigned anyway. So yes — temp file leaks on serialization failure. This contradicts the #H-013 fix intent ("a tracked run does not leak a temp file").

content.text: The SIGTERM handler: `signal.signal` returns the prior handler, which for the default is `signal.SIG_DFL` (an int constant, not callable) or `signal.SIG_IGN`. The `callable(prior)` guard means if the prior handler was `SIG_DFL` (default: terminate process), the chain just returns and does NOT terminate — the process keeps running after SIGTERM instead of dying. That's a behavior change: installing this flush handler converts default-terminate SIGTERM into a no-op (process survives SIGTERM). That's a real defect — a `kill <pid>` (SIGTERM) on a study process would flush traces but then NOT terminate, requiring SIGKILL. Let me verify.

content.text: Confirmed: the artifact serialization failure is caught by `track()` and recorded only as a generic `tracking_error` entry (no `_log.warning`, unlike the start_run path which logs). The provenance artifact silently never attaches to the run, and the temp file leaks. All three findings are solid.

findings.title: SIGTERM trace-flush handler makes the study process unkillable by SIGTERM (swallows default terminate)
findings.claim: After bootstrap_tracing() runs on the main thread, it installs a SIGTERM handler that flushes/shuts down the tracer and then only re-invokes the prior handler `if callable(prior)`. Python's default SIGTERM disposition is signal.SIG_DFL == 0, which is NOT callable, so the chain falls through and the process does NOT terminate on SIGTERM. A `kill <pid>` / `docker stop` / k8s pod SIGTERM flushes traces but leaves the process running, requiring SIGKILL.
findings.why_real: Reproduced directly: with prior=signal.getsignal(SIGTERM)=0 (SIG_DFL, callable()==False), sending os.kill(pid, SIGTERM) flushed but the process printed 'STILL ALIVE AFTER SIGTERM' and exited 0 only because the script ended. In a long-running study this converts graceful SIGTERM into a no-op; orchestrators that send SIGTERM-then-wait hang until their SIGKILL grace timeout. The fix-comment claims the handler 'also flushes+shuts down' but never preserves the default-terminate semantics, silently breaking normal process termination for every traced run.
findings.suggested_fix: After flush/shutdown, if prior is callable invoke it; else replicate the prior disposition: for SIG_DFL re-raise the default action (reset handler to SIG_DFL and os.kill(os.getpid(), signum) or raise SystemExit), for SIG_IGN do nothing. Do not silently return when prior is SIG_DFL.

findings.title: Provenance artifact silently dropped and temp file leaked when a payload holds a hashable-but-non-JSON value
findings.claim: ProvenanceTrail.record() accepts any *hashable* leaf (freeze() only rejects unhashable values), so bytes/datetime/Enum/custom-hashable values in a payload (e.g. a gate evidence dict, line 174 as_recorder) are stored intact and survive unfreeze() unchanged. In _log_trail, json.dump(entries_to_records(...)) then raises TypeError before `path = fh.name` is reached, so (a) the NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) is orphaned on disk with provenance contents, and (b) track() catches the TypeError and records only a generic 'tracking_error' entry — the #G-009 provenance artifact never attaches to the MLflow run.
findings.why_real: Reproduced: trail.record('gate','g', evidence={'token': b'..rawbytes', 'when': datetime(...)}) is accepted by freeze, then json.dumps(entries_to_records(...)) raises 'Object of type bytes is not JSON serializable'. This contradicts both the #H-013 fix intent ('a tracked run does not leak a temp file') — the cleanup try/finally at line 748 is unreachable because `path` is never assigned — and the #G-009 guarantee that an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that produced its metrics. Failure is silent (no _log.warning, unlike the start_run path).
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the temp-file lifecycle in try/finally keyed off the file object (or use delete=True after a fsync/copy), and have _log_trail use a JSON-safe serializer (default=str / reject non-JSON values at record() time). Emit a _log.warning when the provenance artifact cannot be serialized, matching the start_run failure path.

findings.title: Transcript scrubber fails OPEN on any file containing a NUL byte (entire file copied verbatim, no redaction)
findings.claim: Binary classification is `if b"\x00" in raw: data = raw` — a single NUL byte anywhere in the source routes the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch, bypassing every secret/PII redaction pattern. JSONL transcripts routinely embed arbitrary subprocess stdout inside string values, and that stdout can contain a NUL (binary file dumps, `grep`/`cat` of binaries, terminal control bytes, truncated multibyte), so a real transcript file's API tokens, bearer tokens, emails, and operator identity would be committed unredacted.
findings.why_real: Reproduced end-to-end: a source JSONL line containing `[REDACTED]`, `[REDACTED]`, and a NUL byte was copied to the destination completely unredacted (the token and email survived verbatim). The module docstring explicitly claims this branch is 'FAIL CLOSED' and only triggers on 'a genuinely binary blob', but a NUL inside legitimate text content silently defeats the entire scrubber for that file. No committed transcript currently contains a NUL (latent, not yet materialized), but the campaign mandate commits transcripts on every commit, so the leak is one binary-output subprocess away.
findings.suggested_fix: Do not treat a stray NUL as 'binary'. Decode with errors="replace" and ALWAYS run _scrub on text (the existing replace-decode already handles invalid bytes); reserve verbatim copy for files whose content-type is genuinely non-text (e.g. known-binary suffixes), or strip NUL bytes before scrubbing rather than skipping redaction.

content.text:
I completed an adversarial observability/provenance review of the hc-assay engine and found three genuine, reproduced production-readiness defects.

Summary of findings (all verified by running code, not speculation):

1. **K-OBS-1 (high) — SIGTERM handler makes traced processes unkillable.** `src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py:148-156`. The trace-flush SIGTERM handler only chains to the prior handler `if callable(prior)`, but the default disposition `SIG_DFL` is the int `0` (not callable), so SIGTERM flushes traces and then returns without terminating. Reproduced: process survives SIGTERM. Breaks `kill`/`docker stop`/k8s graceful shutdown for every traced run.

2. **K-OBS-2 (high) — provenance artifact silently dropped + temp file leaked on a hashable-but-non-JSON payload.** `src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:733-756`. `freeze()` accepts any hashable leaf (bytes/datetime/Enum), so such values reach `_log_trail` where `json.dump(...)` raises `TypeError` before `path = fh.name`. The `NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)` then leaks on disk (defeating the #H-013 cleanup, which is unreachable) and the #G-009 provenance artifact never attaches to the MLflow run — caught silently by `track()` as a generic `tracking_error`. Reproduced with a `bytes`/`datetime` evidence payload.

3. **K-OBS-3 (medium) — transcript scrubber fails OPEN on any NUL byte.** `scripts/capture_transcripts.py:240-243`. A single NUL byte anywhere in a source transcript routes the entire file to the verbatim branch, bypassing all secret/PII redaction — directly contradicting the docstring's "FAIL CLOSED" claim. Reproduced: a line with an `sk-ant-` token and an email plus a NUL was copied unredacted. Latent (no committed transcript currently has a NUL) but reachable on the next binary-subprocess-output capture.

Areas I checked and found clean: MLflow run lifecycle (start/log/end status, FAILED termination, TOCTOU experiment-create race, orphaned-run cleanup), NaN/inf metric handling (accepted by mlflow 3.13 and swallowed anyway), env-var int parsing, and logging for secret leakage (no params/payloads are logged). The import-time binding of `ASSAY_EXPERIMENT`/`ASSAY_TRACING_PORT` is an asymmetry with the call-time `ASSAY_TRACKING_URI` but is low-impact and effectively by-design, so I did not report it.
